turns.ai: Hello, welcome to the Airport. How can I help you today?

eval.improvement_suggestion: Maintain the natural flow and concise questioning approach. Ensure all responses strictly adhere to the scenario-specific opening rules without adding extraneous explanations.

turns.student: "Hi, I need to check in for my flight to Brazil, please. Can you help me?" (Confused about the airport terms, mixing Portuguese words like "Brasil" with English.)
turns.ai: Certainly! I can help you with that. May I see your passport, please?

eval.improvement_suggestion: Ask for flight details next to progress the interaction naturally.

turns.student: Okay... passport? Where? I... I think I have it. Wait, do I need to show it now? *confused*
turns.ai: Yes, please. I need to see your passport to check you in. Do you have it with you?

eval.improvement_suggestion: Maintain natural flow by keeping responses concise and focused on the scenario.

turns.student: "Ah, yes! I have it. Can I show it now?" (I think... maybe I need to open the bag? *confused*)
turns.ai: Yes, please. Whenever you're ready, I need to see it to proceed with your check-in. Do you need help opening your bag?

eval.improvement_suggestion: Maintain concise, action-focused responses without additional explanations.

turns.student: No, thank you. I can do it myself. Where do I put it?
turns.ai: You can hand it to me, please. Do you have any bags to check?

eval.improvement_suggestion: Response is natural and compliant. No changes needed.

turns.student: "Ah, yes! I have a bag. Can I check it in? Or is it 'check in'?" (confused, looking at the bag)
turns.ai: Yes, you can check your bag in. Is this the only bag you'll be checking?

eval.improvement_suggestion: Maintain concise, question-based responses without added explanations.

turns.student: "Yup, this is the only bag. / É só essa mala?" (Confused, looking at the bag again.)
turns.ai: Okay, perfect. I'll take your bag now. Can I see your passport again to confirm the details?

eval.improvement_suggestion: Remove the 'Okay, perfect' compliment and make the response more direct without evaluative language.

turns.student: "Por favor, sim. Why again? I showed before." (confused, repeating "sim" and "por favor")
turns.ai: I just need to double-check the passport details to print your baggage tag, sir. Is that okay?

eval.improvement_suggestion: Consider simplifying the response to focus more directly on the single question, while maintaining procedural accuracy.
